reforms.description: The federal government announced the Universal Broadband Fund, which will provide CAD 1.75 billion for broadband projects, including CAD 50 million specifically for mobile projects benefiting Indigenous communities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving broadband access supports economic growth and innovation by enhancing connectivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund has been announced and is currently in operation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant investment in infrastructure aimed at improving access to broadband, which is crucial for economic development.
reforms.source_quote: In November 2020, the federal government announced the Universal Broadband Fund. This will provide CAD 1.75 billion for broadband projects.

reforms.description: Canada's federal government plans to adopt a quality-of-life framework with an economic lens to guide policy towards long-term inclusive and environmentally sustainable growth, addressing the lack of an official framework.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: A quality-of-life framework can enhance policy coherence and support sustainable economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The framework has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform aims to create a comprehensive framework for guiding economic and social policies, representing a significant shift in approach.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government should follow through on its plan to adopt a quality-of-life (well-being) framework with an economic lens to help guide the policy agenda to enhance long-term inclusive and environmentally sustainable growth.

reforms.description: The federal government committed to creating a task force to guide an Action Plan on Women in the Economy, aiming to address gender inequality and improve women's economic participation.
reforms.package_name: Action Plan on Women in the Economy
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving women's economic participation can enhance overall economic productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The task force has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, this reform is more focused and does not represent a system-wide change.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government recently committed to creating a task force to guide an Action Plan on Women in the Economy.

reforms.description: The federal government has implemented a National Housing Strategy, a ten-year plan designed to reduce housing need for 530,000 households and chronic homelessness by half by 2027-2028, with an initial budget of CAD 40 billion, later expanded to over CAD 55 billion.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Addressing housing needs can stimulate economic activity and support community development.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy has been implemented and is currently in operation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a major investment in housing infrastructure and social policy.
reforms.source_quote: The ten-year, initially CAD 40 billion plan is designed to reduce or eliminate housing need for some 530 000 households.

reforms.description: The federal government launched the Rapid Housing Initiative, a CAD 1 billion measure aimed at the rapid construction of up to 3,000 permanent affordable housing units.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Rapidly increasing the supply of affordable housing can stimulate local economies and support community stability.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been launched and is currently in operation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant but narrower in scope compared to larger housing strategies.
reforms.source_quote: In 2020 the federal government also launched the Rapid Housing Initiative, a CAD 1 billion dollar measure for the rapid construction of as many as 3 000 permanent affordable housing units.

reforms.description: The federal government announced an increase of CAD 12 billion to the existing Rental Construction Financing Initiative, aimed at providing low-interest loans and mortgage insurance for developers of purpose-built rental housing, facilitating the construction of approximately 28,500 units.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing financing for rental construction can enhance housing supply and stimulate economic activity.
reforms.status_evidence: The increase in funding has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant but does not represent a major overhaul of existing policies.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government just announced its intent to boost the existing Rental Construction Financing Initiative by CAD 12 billion over seven years.

reforms.description: The Reaching Home: Canada’s Homelessness Strategy was launched to provide direct financial support to communities to address local homelessness needs, with a goal to reduce chronic homelessness by half by 2027-2028.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Providing support to address homelessness can improve community well-being and economic stability.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy has been launched and is currently in operation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This strategy represents a significant commitment to addressing homelessness at a national level.
reforms.source_quote: Launched in April 2019, Reaching Home: Canada’s Homelessness Strategy is intended to support the most vulnerable Canadians in maintaining safe, stable and affordable housing.

reforms.description: The federal government has implemented a flexible funding system for First Nations communities, allowing for ten-year grants instead of annual appropriations, aiming to provide more predictable and sustained funding.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Providing more predictable funding can enhance the capacity of First Nations communities to engage in long-term planning and development.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding system has been implemented and is currently in operation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant for First Nations but does not represent a system-wide change.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government funds First Nations communities with various flexible funding instruments... began to offer ten-year grants.
